data.title: +$5,100 Gold is Making These Ounces in the Ground a Lot More Interesting
data.description: Lake Victoria Gold Ltd. on behalf of Equity-Insider.com announced on March 6, 2026, that several gold producers achieved remarkable free cash flow margins in the fourth quarter of 2025. During this period, the industry's average all-in sustaining costs ranged from $925 to $1,025 per ounce. Moreover, the realized prices for gold surpassed this cost range, resulting in substantial profits for these producers.

data.title: Liquidia Analysts Boost Their Forecasts After Q4 Earnings
data.description: Liquidia Corp (NASDAQ: LQDA) announced its fourth-quarter results, revealing quarterly earnings of 15 cents per share. This fell short of the anticipated 17 cents per share according to analysts. However, the company reported quarterly sales of $92.021 million, surpassing the expected $83.634 million. CEO Dr. Roger Jeffs expressed optimism for the future, highlighting the rapid acceptance of YUTREPIA® in clinical settings. This product is considered one of the most successful specialty drug launches in the past five years across various therapeutic areas. The adoption of YUTREPIA® continues to expand in conditions such as PAH and PH-ILD.

data.description: The numbers released fell below expectations and sparked tension between the two parties leading up to the upcoming midterm elections.

data.title: HomePlunge Unveils the Bella: A Compact, Affordable Cold Plunge Chiller for Every Home
data.description: A renowned brand known for its award-winning bathtub cold plunge products is introducing a new addition to its lineup. The latest chiller is smaller, quieter, and more convenient, set to be showcased at the HFA Show 2026 in San Diego. Originating from San Francisco, HomePlunge has launched its newest cold plunge chiller model, which is set to debut publicly at the event. This marks an exciting evolution of their product line, as the brand continues to innovate in the industry.

data.title: Enterprise Products Partners L.P. 2025 Letter to Investors Now Available
data.description: Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (NYSE: EPD) released its 2025 Letter to Investors, accessible on their website under the Investors tab, highlighting its position as a top publicly traded partnership. As a leading provider of midstream energy services in North America, the partnership caters to producers and consumers of natural gas, NGLs, crude oil, refined products, and petrochemicals. For more information, visit www.enterpriseproducts.com.

data.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On Ciena Following Upbeat Q1 Earnings
data.description: Ciena Corp (NYSE: CIEN) reported positive financial results for Q1, surpassing market expectations. The adjusted earnings per share stood at $1.35, exceeding the anticipated $1.17 per share. The company's sales reached $1.427 billion, higher than the estimated $1.399 billion. Additionally, Ciena revised its FY2026 sales forecast from $5.700 billion-$6.100 billion to $5.900 billion-$6.300 billion. CEO Gary Smith attributed the success to strong execution and growing demand for AI-enabled solutions. He highlighted the company's leading technology and robust customer relationships positioning them well for future growth in the AI-driven networking sector. Ciena remains focused on providing long-term value to its stakeholders through continuous investment and strategic execution. For detailed information, refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Invitation & Agenda: Orexo R&D Day on March 24
data.description: A company based in Uppsala, Sweden, Orexo AB (Publ.), has announced it will be hosting an R&D Day on March 24, 2026. The event, which will be held at the Royal Swedish Academy of Engineering Sciences (IVA) on Grev Turegatan, is open to investors, analysts, and media personnel. Orexo AB's stock is listed as STO: ORX and OTCQX: ORXOY.

data.title: FTAI Aviation Taps New CFO And Accounting Chief
data.description: Shares of FTAI Aviation Ltd. (NASDAQ: FTAI) dropped on Friday after the company announced key leadership changes. Nicholas McAleese has been named the new CFO, and Michael Hazan is now the Chief Accounting Officer. Both appointments are effective immediately, replacing Eun (Angela) Nam who is leaving to explore opportunities at another public company outside the aviation sector. McAleese, previously the Senior VP of FP&A, joined FTAI in 2022 and has been instrumental in developing core corporate finance functions. Hazan, who joined in 2017 as the Senior VP and Controller, has been pivotal in accounting, financial reporting, and operational activities. The company's recent earnings report for the fourth quarter of 2025, released on Feb. 25, 2026, revealed EPS of $1.08, falling short of the estimated $1.29. This has led to increased scrutiny of the stock. For the complete story, visit Benzinga.com.
